title: 10 best herbal capsules for allergies
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for allergies are dietary supplements that contain a variety of natural herbs and botanicals, which can help to alleviate symptoms of allergies. These capsules can be a natural alternative to over-the-counter or prescription medications, and can be effective for treating seasonal allergies, hay fever, and other environmental allergies. Some examples of herbs commonly found in these capsules include nettle, butterbur, and quercetin. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and congestion, and alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. For example, a person who suffers from seasonal allergies and takes herbal capsules may find that they are able to spend more time outdoors without experiencing symptoms, or that they are able to reduce their reliance on prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsule helps with allergies because it contains anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that work to alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The herb, which is rich in flavonoids and alkaloids, works by reducing the production of histamine, a compound that the immune system releases in response to allergens. By inhibiting the release of histamine, yarrow capsules can help to prevent allergic reactions and provide relief from allergy symptoms. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow capsules can reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and respiratory tract, alleviating allergy symptoms, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antihistamine: Yarrow contains alkaloids that act as natural antihistamines, helping to relieve symptoms like sneezing, itching, and runny nose, by blocking the release of histamine in the body.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow, resulting from tannins, help tighten and soothe mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and discomfort associated with allergies.
Immune-modulating: Yarrow regulates the immune response, reducing allergic reactions, as it contains volatile oils, such as azulene and chamazulene, that help balance the immune system.
Analgesic: Yarrow has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headaches, sore throats, and other discomforts related to allergies, due to the presence of lactones and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in reducing allergy symptoms.
Leaves: Yarrow leaves contain flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a good source of essential oils and have been used in traditional medicine to treat allergies due to their anti-allergic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and other contaminants, then rinse them gently.
Dry the yarrow plants in a well-ventilated area away from direct sunlight for 1-2 weeks, or until they become crispy and crumble easily.
Grind the dried yarrow plants into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500mg of the yarrow powder and fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed yarrow capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place, and take 1-2 capsules daily as needed for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic capsule helps with allergies because it has natural anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. The active compound in garlic, allicin, can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate symptoms such as congestion, runny nose, and sneezing. Additionally, garlic has been shown to enhance the immune system's ability to fight off allergens, reducing the severity and frequency of allergy attacks. Furthermore, garlic's antioxidant properties can help protect the body from free radical damage, supporting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and airways, alleviating allergy symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include allicin, ajoene, and other organosulfur compounds.

Immune-boosting: Garlic can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off allergens and reducing the severity of allergy symptoms. The immune-boosting properties of garlic are attributed to its high concentration of vitamin C, vitamin B6, and selenium.

Antihistamine: Garlic has natural antihistamine properties that can help alleviate allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The active constituents responsible for this property include quercetin and other flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Garlic has potent antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by allergens. The antioxidant properties of garlic are attributed to its high concentration of polyphenols, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds.

Anti-inflammatory: Garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and airways, alleviating allergy symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include allicin, ajoene, and other organosulfur compounds.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: The bulbs of garlic plants are commonly used to make medicinal tea for allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: Garlic leaves are used in tea to alleviate allergy symptoms as they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and histamine production.
Flowers: Garlic flowers are rich in antioxidants and can be used in tea to help reduce allergic reactions and boost the immune system.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 cloves of minced garlic, 1 tablespoon of organic honey, and 2 teaspoons of lemon juice in a bowl, letting it sit for 10 minutes to activate the garlic's medicinal properties.
Blend 1 cup of dried chamomile flowers and 1/2 cup of nettle leaves in a coffee grinder until they form a fine powder.
Mix the garlic mixture with 1 cup of the herbal powder, gradually adding water until a dough-like consistency is reached.
Form the dough into small capsule-sized pieces, placing them on a parchment-lined baking sheet and allowing them to dry for 24 hours.
Store the dried garlic-herb capsules in an airtight container for up to one month, taking 1-2 capsules daily as a natural remedy for allergies.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly weed capsule helps with allergies because it contains alkaloids and other beneficial compounds that can reduce inflammation and histamine production in the body. These natural chemicals help to alleviate symptoms of allergies such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. Additionally, butterfly weed has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body to better fight off allergens and reduce the severity of allergic reactions. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe irritated membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Overall, butterfly weed capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage allergies and improve quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Butterfly weed contains compounds such as alkaloids and cardiac glycosides that help reduce inflammation, which is important for allergies as it can alleviate symptoms like swelling and redness caused by allergic reactions.
Immune-boosting: The plant has immune-enhancing properties due to the presence of various antioxidants and phytochemicals, helping strengthen the body's defense against allergens.

Antihistamine: Butterfly weed contains chemicals that can act as natural antihistamines, blocking the action of histamine, a compound released during an allergic reaction that causes symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny nose.
Antitussive: The soothing properties of butterfly weed can help suppress coughing, providing relief from allergy-induced respiratory symptoms.
Expectorant: Butterfly weed can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion caused by allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterfly weed plant contain natural chemicals that can help reduce inflammation and histamine production, making it useful for treating allergies.
Leaves: The leaves of the butterfly weed plant are rich in medicinal compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-allergic properties.

Flowers: The bright orange flowers of the butterfly weed plant are not only beautiful but also contain medicinal properties. They are rich in nectar, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the butterfly weed plant contain essential oils and other medicinal compounds that can help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 dried butterfly weed leaves and grind them into a fine powder, taking approximately 10-15 minutes.
Mix the ground butterfly weed with 1 tablespoon of organic coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax, heating the mixture on low temperature until fully combined.
Allow the mixture to cool down before adding 1/4 teaspoon of lecithin granules, stirring until evenly distributed.
Fill size 000 empty gelatin capsules with the cooled butterfly weed mixture, using a capsule filling machine or manually.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, and take 1-2 capsules per day as needed for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo capsule helps with allergies because it contains active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from allergy symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion. Additionally, wild indigo supports the immune system, helping the body to better fight off allergens and reducing the severity of allergy symptoms. Overall, wild indigo capsules offer a natural and effective solution for managing allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild indigo capsules contain isoflavones, which help reduce inflammation in the body, providing relief from allergy symptoms such as swelling and redness.
Immune-boosting: The capsules have alkaloids like indican, which support the immune system and help the body fight off allergens.
Antimicrobial: Wild indigo contains tannins that have antimicrobial properties, aiding in preventing infections that can occur due to allergies.
Antioxidant: The capsules contain polyphenols, which act as antioxidants, helping protect the body from free radicals and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate allergies.
Expectorant: Wild indigo has saponins that act as expectorants, helping clear mucus from the respiratory tract and alleviating congestion caused by allergies.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, wild indigo flowers help reduce inflammation and histamine production, providing allergy relief.
Leaves: High in rotenoids, wild indigo leaves have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can alleviate allergy symptoms.
Roots: Containing indigoquinone and indigotin, wild indigo roots provide immune-boosting and anti-inflammatory benefits, making them useful for allergy relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 parts wild indigo leaves and 1 part each of nettle, plantain, and goldenrod herbs, ensuring they are properly identified and sourced from a safe location.
Thoroughly clean and dry the herbs, then finely chop or grind them to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 cup of the ground herbal mixture with 1.5 cups of a binder such as magnesium stearate or leucine, then blend well.
Using a capsule filling machine, fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the herbal mixture, aiming for a consistent 500-600mg dosage per capsule.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dry, and dark location, and take 1-2 capsules, 2-3 times daily as needed for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Flame of the forest capsule helps with allergies because it contains natural herbal extracts that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and block the release of histamines in the body, which are responsible for causing allergic reactions. By taking flame of the forest capsules, the body's immune system can become stronger, and its response to allergens can be reduced, providing relief from symptoms such as sneezing, coughing, and watery eyes. Additionally, the herbal extracts in the capsules can help to soothe and heal inflamed respiratory tracts, further alleviating allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Flame of the forest capsules contain compounds such as lupeol and β-sitosterol that help reduce inflammation, which is important for allergies as they often cause inflammation in the nasal passages and airways.
Antihistamine: The bark of the flame of the forest tree contains alkaloids that act as natural antihistamines, helping to alleviate symptoms of allergies such as sneezing, itching, and runny nose.

Immune-modulating: Flame of the forest capsules can help regulate the immune system, which is crucial for managing allergies since they are caused by an overactive immune response. The active constituents responsible for this property include polysaccharides and triterpenoids.
Antioxidant: The plant contains various antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and tannins, that help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate allergy symptoms.

Anti-asthmatic: Flame of the forest capsules may help alleviate asthmatic symptoms associated with allergies due to their bronchodilatory and expectorant properties. The presence of alkaloids and triterpenes contributes to these effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The bright red flowers of the flame of the forest tree contain high levels of antioxidants and have been traditionally used to treat allergies and inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of the flame of the forest plant are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for soothing allergy symptoms.
Bark: The bark of the flame of the forest tree contains tannins that have been shown to have anti-allergic properties, making it a common ingredient in medicinal teas for allergies.
Seeds: The seeds of the flame of the forest plant are rich in essential fatty acids and have been used to treat allergies, particularly those affecting the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of flame of the forest bark and 250 grams of dried ginger, then grind them separately into a fine powder.
Mix the two powders in a bowl, adding 100 grams of honey and 50 grams of lemon juice to create a thick paste.
Divide the paste into equal portions and shape them into small capsules using gelatin capsule molds.
Allow the capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours before storing them in an airtight container.
Take one capsule twice daily, preferably after meals, for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower capsule helps with allergies because it contains high levels of echinacea, a powerful antioxidant that boosts the immune system and reduces inflammation. This herbal supplement has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including allergies. By reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, echinacea helps alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. Additionally, echinacea has been shown to have antihistamine properties, which can help further reduce allergic reactions. Overall, eastern purple coneflower capsules can provide natural relief for allergy sufferers and improve their quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation in the nasal passages and respiratory tract. Active constituents include alkamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid.

Immune-boosting: Echinacea purpurea can help strengthen the immune system, making it more effective at fighting off allergens and reducing the severity of allergy symptoms. Active constituents include alkamides, polysaccharides, and glycoproteins.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Echinacea purpurea help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to inflammation and allergic reactions. Active constituents include cichoric acid, caftaric acid, and chlorogenic acid.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of Echinacea purpurea can help prevent secondary infections that may occur as a result of allergies, further supporting overall immune function and reducing the risk of complications. Active constituents include alkamides, essential oils, and polysaccharides.

Analgesic: Echinacea purpurea has analgesic (pain-relieving) properties, which can help alleviate discomfort associated with allergy symptoms, such as headaches, sore throats, and sinus pressure. Active constituents include alkamides, essential oils, and polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of eastern purple coneflower are used in medicinal tea to provide allergy relief by reducing inflammation.
Leaves: High in antioxidants, coneflower leaves are used in teas to help alleviate allergy symptoms by supporting the immune system.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, coneflower seeds are used in medicinal teas to help soothe irritated nasal passages and ease breathing.
Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, coneflower roots are used in teas to provide allergy relief by reducing inflammation in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried Eastern purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are organically grown and free from pesticides.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for approximately 5 minutes.
Measure out 500mg of the ground coneflower powder for each capsule, using a scale to ensure accurate dosage.
Fill size "00" vegetarian capsules with the measured powder, using a capsule filling machine or manually, and store in airtight containers.
Take 1-2 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, for allergy relief, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsule helps with allergies because it contains bioflavonoids and tannins that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with allergies. Eyebright herb has been traditionally used to soothe irritated mucous membranes and alleviate symptoms of allergies such as watery eyes, sneezing, and runny nose. Moreover, eyebright may also have antihistamine effects, which can help relieve allergic reactions by blocking the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic symptoms. Therefore, taking eyebright capsules may provide relief from allergy symptoms and improve overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for allergies as they often cause inflammation in the eyes, nose, and throat. Active constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids.

Antihistamine: Eyebright contains antihistamine properties that can help alleviate symptoms of allergies by blocking the release of histamines, chemicals that the immune system produces in response to allergens.
Astringent: Eyebright has astringent properties, which can help to tighten and tone mucous membranes, reducing the production of mucus and providing relief from allergic symptoms.

Antimicrobial: Eyebright capsules have antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections caused by allergies, such as sinus infections. Active constituents include tannins and volatile oils.

Immune-boosting: Eyebright can help boost the immune system, which is important for managing allergies as a strong immune system can help reduce the severity of allergic reactions. Active constituents include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and sterols.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in bioflavonoids and tannins, the eyebright flowers are used in medicinal tea for allergies due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Leaves: High in phenolic acids, the leaves of the eyebright plant are included in allergy relief teas because of their antioxidant and anti-histamine effects.
Seeds: Containing essential oils and alkaloids, eyebright seeds are used in tea blends for allergies, as they help reduce inflammation and mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Eyebright herb and 1 teaspoon of grinded Echinacea root.
Combine the herbs with 1 cup of distilled water in a pot, simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool, then mix in 500mg of empty vegetarian capsules.
Fill the capsules with the cooled herbal mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the finished Eyebright capsules in a cool, dry place, and take as directed for allergy relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsule helps with allergies because it contains a natural compound called Anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and alleviate allergy symptoms. Fennel is also known for its ability to break up congestion, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, fennel has antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can help boost the immune system and protect the body from harmful pathogens. By taking fennel capsules, individuals may experience relief from symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes, making it a natural and effective remedy for allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel capsules can reduce inflammation, which is important for allergies as they often cause inflammation in the nasal passages and airways. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as anethole, limonene, and anisic aldehyde.

Antihistamine: Fennel contains compounds that can act as natural antihistamines, helping to alleviate symptoms of allergies such as sneezing, itching, and watery eyes. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids like rutin and quercetin.

Bronchodilator: Fennel can help relax and widen the airways, making it easier to breathe. This property is particularly beneficial for allergies that cause respiratory issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils like anethole and fenchone.

Antioxidant: Fennel has potent antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. This property is important for allergies as it can help reduce the overall severity of symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids like rutin and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids like rosmarinic acid.

Immune-boosting: Fennel can help strengthen the immune system, which can help the body better manage allergic reactions. This property is important for allergies as a strong immune system can help reduce the frequency and severity of allergy symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include vitamin C, vitamin A, and essential minerals like calcium, potassium, and magnesium.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and histamine production, providing relief for allergies.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are a great source of vitamin C and can help boost the immune system, reducing the severity of allergy symptoms.
Stems: Fennel stems contain essential oils that have been shown to have anti-allergic properties, making them useful in treating allergies.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes have a high concentration of anethole, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the release of histamines, providing relief for allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried fennel seeds and 0.5-1 teaspoon of local honey.
Grind the fennel seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground fennel seeds with honey to create a consistent paste.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the fennel-honey mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours before storing them in airtight containers at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with allergies because it contains active compounds that can reduce inflammation and mucus production in the respiratory tract. The main active ingredient in licorice, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a compound that plays a key role in allergic reactions. By blocking the action of histamine, licorice capsules can help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as sneezing, runny nose, and watery eyes. Additionally, licorice has expectorant properties, which means it can help clear phlegm and mucus from the airways, providing further relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice helps reduce inflammation in the airways, which is important for allergies as they often cause inflammation and swelling. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Immune system modulation: Licorice can help regulate the immune system, reducing the overreaction that leads to allergic responses. The active constituents that contribute to this property are glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin.

Anti-histamine: Licorice has natural antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies, such as sneezing, runny nose, and itchy eyes. The active constituent responsible for this property is liquiritin.

Expectorant: Licorice can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing associated with allergies. The active constituent that gives licorice this property is glycyrrhizin.
Soothing properties: Licorice can soothe irritated mucous membranes, reducing discomfort caused by allergies. The active constituents that contribute to this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice capsule plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate allergic reactions.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice capsule plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce histamine production and alleviate allergy symptoms.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice capsule plant contain antioxidants that can help strengthen the immune system and reduce inflammation caused by allergies.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice capsule plant are a good source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried licorice root and size "00" empty capsules, ensuring the licorice root is organic and the capsules are vegetarian.
Measure 2000mg of dried licorice root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill each empty capsule with 250mg of the licorice root powder, using a capsule-filling machine or manually.
Seal the capsules and store in an airtight container, keeping them in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.
Take one capsule up to three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English ivy capsule helps with allergies because it contains extracts from the English ivy plant, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as nasal congestion, sneezing, and itchy eyes. Additionally, English ivy has been found to have expectorant effects, which can help clear mucus from the respiratory tract. Furthermore, it can also help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Overall, English ivy capsules can provide natural relief for allergy symptoms, and may be a good alternative for those who prefer to avoid over-the-counter medications or have experienced side effects from them.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English ivy capsules can reduce inflammation in the airways, which is important for allergies as it can help alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and saponins.

Antihistamine: English ivy contains properties that can act as a natural antihistamine, helping to reduce the production of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions. The active constituents responsible for this property include Hederagenin and Hederasaponin B.

Expectorant: English ivy can help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, which is important for allergies as it can help clear blocked airways and reduce congestion. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and Flavonoids.

Immune-boosting: English ivy can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for allergies as it can help the body better fight off allergens and reduce the severity of symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and Flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: English ivy has antimicrobial properties that can help protect against infections, which is important for allergies as it can help prevent secondary infections that can occur as a result of allergic reactions. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins and Flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the English ivy capsule plant contain active compounds that help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and mucus production.
Stems: Ivy stems have been used in medicinal teas to treat allergies due to their antihistamine properties, which can help relieve symptoms such as sneezing and itching.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the English ivy capsule plant contain saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them useful in treating allergies.

Flowers: Ivy flowers are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which have been shown to have anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for allergies.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 English ivy leaves and let them wilt for 24 hours to reduce their bitterness.
Grind the wilted leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500mg of the ivy powder with an excipient, such as microcrystalline cellulose, at a 1:1 ratio.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the ivy-excipient mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place, and take one capsule three times daily for allergy relief.
